question: What percentage of documented cluster munition casualties since 1960 were civilians, and which organization provided this data?
answer: Approximately 94% of roughly 23,000 documented casualties were civilians, according to the Landmine & Cluster Munition Monitor.

video: Cluster munitions have caused 94% of global cluster munition casualties since 1960, according to data from the Landmine & Cluster Munition Monitor, 2021.
text: Approximately 94% of the roughly 23,000 documented cluster munition deaths worldwide since the 1960s have been civilians.

question: What key difference exists in casualty demographics between the Kosovo conflict and other cluster munition incidents globally?
answer: In Kosovo, 62.5% of cluster munition fatalities were boys under 18 years old, contrasting with the global civilian casualty rate of 94%.

video: In Kosovo, cluster bomb deaths were predominantly boys under 18, accounting for 62.5% of the fatalities.
text: In Kosovo after the Yugoslav Wars, 62.5% of cluster munition casualties were boys under 18 years old.
Approximately 94% of the roughly 23,000 documented cluster munition deaths worldwide since the 1960s have been civilians.
